title: Secure Token Exchange for Agentic Platform Integration — Development Plan
body: Here is a more granular document of what needs to be implemented to support of secure JWT exchange.
author: AdamCollins

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a design/implementation guide describing how ProjectSight will securely exchange JWT tokens for the new Agentic Platform integration, introducing a low-privilege `ProjectSight-Agents` token alongside the existing `ProjectSight-Web` token and outlining the work needed across the Dev Console, Web, External API, and MCP server.

**Changes:**
- New documentation file describing the token exchange architecture, required work items, PoC plan, and out-of-scope items.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: @kcostenbader ,  please comment on if we need this part before release to production
review_comments.author: wenhong-at-trimble

review_comments.body: @wenhong-at-trimble Yes, this is absolutely needed. We also must have opt-in operation security before releasing.
review_comments.author: kcostenbader

review_comments.body:
@AdamCollins @edgarherrador @wenhong-at-trimble @Daniel-Weng-TC1 

Just to summarize for myself ... 

- Users have a "regular" JWT (ProjectSight-Web-Prod with no (required) scopes)
- For any AI (area) in the app we generate a "agent" JWT [for the user] (maybe ProjectSight-Agent-Prod with "context" scope of Drawing (if run from the Drawing area)).
- The MCP server can exchange **ANY TID JWT** for a "mcp" JWT [for the user] (maybe ProjeSight-MCP-Prod with a "context" if the original token had a context scope.
- This allows the MCP server to execute API on behave of the user and limited to only what MCP's that user is allowed to use.
review_comments.author: rroesler

issue_comments.body: @kcostenbader @rroesler Trying out creating docs in markdown so that Agents can review and implement. Please feel free to leave any comments.
issue_comments.author: AdamCollins

issue_comments.body:
@AdamCollins Looks good.  

In mobile we have build a Description folder with user level description of the system mostly extracted from help documents.

And a Technical folder with technical "how are things implemented and structured" 

And a feedback loops where the review agent is encouraged to enhance these. 

And we have a ./Plans (folder) were one can create a Plan like what you have here and then continue work on it over several sessions. 

The Jira / Plan disconnect is probably our next "worry"  

But, yes, I like using the AI to help plan our and search out various implementation options and to document pit falls in the work.  

One aspect of these approach I have not quite figured out is how to stop the plans and descriptions from becoming their own maintenance and context window nightmare.   Has not happened yet but it is sometimes close
issue_comments.author: rroesler

issue_comments.body: @AdamCollins @kcostenbader @rroesler , we are also looking at the MCP security best practices : https://github.com/Trimble-Agentic/mcp-hosting/blob/main/docs/06-security.md
issue_comments.author: wenhong-at-trimble

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,167 @@ +# Secure Token Exchange for Agentic Platform Integration — Implementation Guide + +In order to use the Agentic Platform Component in ProjectSight, we need to provide a TID user token to the client side for the component to authenticate the user. We also need to provide the ProjectSight MCP with a user token that enables it to call the external ProjectSight API in order to complete requests. + +We are currently able to provide a TID token of the user under the ProjectSight-Web Dev Console application, but this token has a very high level of privilege and can be used to call any ProjectSight API on behalf of the user, which represents a security risk if directly exposed to the client-side/Agentic Platform Component. + +To mitigate this risk, we will introduce a new Dev Console Application `ProjectSight-Agents` that is able to authenticate the identity of the user without allowing the token to be used directly against the ProjectSight API. + +The MCP server will then exchange the `ProjectSight-Agents` token for a `ProjectSight-Web` token via a new restricted endpoint in the ProjectSight Web API, which it can then use to call the external API. This way, the client-side component only ever has access to the low-privilege `ProjectSight-Agents` token, and can never call the ProjectSight API directly. + +[Architecture Diagrams](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CRjsD_yKHD9btZxmfHnSnzZsSuccEdNC/view?usp=sharing) + + +## Required Work + +### 1. Create Application in Dev Console +Create a new `ProjectSight-Agents-Pre-Prod` application in the Staging Dev Console. +An equivalent application `ProjectSight-Agents-Prod` will also need to be created in the Production Dev Console before release. +> From now on, I will omit the `Prod/Pre-Prod` from both applications as `ProjectSight-Web`/`ProjectSight-Agents` for simplicity. + +The application must be marked restricted so no unauthorized party can subscribe. + + +### 2. ProjectSight Generating ProjectSight-Agents Tokens for Users + +Currently, when a user authenticates with ProjectSight via Trimble Identity, their `ProjectSight-Web` token is cached in IgnitionMaster (via `s_enc_set_user_tid_tokencache_v4`). This cached token can be retrieved at any time via `IdentityService.GetTokenAsync`. + +To obtain a `ProjectSight-Agents` token for the user, ProjectSight will need to perform a token exchange against TID's `/oauth/token` endpoint using the `ProjectSight-Web` client credentials in the Authorization header and the user's cached ID token as the `subject_token`: +``` +POST https://[stage.]id.trimblecloud.com/oauth/token +Authorization: Basic +Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded + +grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange +&subject_token= +&subject_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:jwt +&requested_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:jwt +&scope=ProjectSight-Agents agents +``` +This will result in a new JWT token being created with roughly the following structure: + +```jsonc +{ + // Issued by ProjectSight-Web + "client_id": "", + // For this user + "sub": "", + // For an audience of ProjectSight-Agents application & Agentic Service + "aud": [ + "", + "", + ], + "scope": "ProjectSight-Agents agents", +} +``` + +This allows us to generate a token with an audience of `ProjectSight-Agents` without the user being forced to log in again. + +We will also need to add a Web API endpoint that allows the Agentic Platform to retrieve the `ProjectSight-Agents` token. + +```mermaid +sequenceDiagram + participant Browser as Browser + participant PSWeb as ProjectSight Web + participant TID as Trimble Identity + + Browser->>PSWeb: GET /AgentToken + PSWeb->>TID: POST /oauth/token
grant_type=token-exchange
subject_token={user id_token}
scope=ProjectSight-Agents + TID-->>PSWeb: Agents JWT + PSWeb-->>Browser: Agents JWT +``` + + +### 3. ProjectSight External API Token Exchange Endpoint +Allow the ProjectSight MCP server to securely exchange a `ProjectSight-Agents` token for a `ProjectSight-Web` token that can be used to call the external API. + +- Add a new endpoint to the external API `POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken` that takes in a `ProjectSight-Agents` token and returns a `ProjectSight-Web` token for the corresponding User TID. + +We **must ensure** that this endpoint is only callable by the MCP Service. We can enforce this by only accepting calls from a well-known MCP Service application ID. This can be stored within the MCP ExternalService table. + +```mermaid +sequenceDiagram + participant MCP as ProjectSight MCP + participant PSExtAPI as PS External API + participant IdentityService as IdentityService + + MCP->>PSExtAPI: POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken
x-jwt-assertion: {Agents JWT} + PSExtAPI->>PSExtAPI: Validate token
aud=ProjectSight-Agents, sub=TrimbleID + PSExtAPI->>IdentityService: GetTokenAsync(TrimbleID) + IdentityService-->>PSExtAPI: ProjectSight-Web access token + PSExtAPI-->>MCP: ProjectSight-Web access token +``` + + +### 4. MCP Server Token Exchange +Upon receiving an initial request from the Agentic Service, the MCP server must call the `POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken` endpoint and use the returned token for the duration of the session. + +This endpoint must only accept Agent tokens where the `client_id` is from a well-known application ID: +- `ProjectSight-Web` +- (optional) `Agent-Service` if we want to support calling the PS MCP server from tokens generated on the Agentic Platform portal. + +> **Note:** We don't need to include `ProjectSight-Agents` in the list of accepted application IDs because when exchanging these tokens per Section 2, the `client_id = ProjectSight-Web`. However, including it would not cause any issues. + + +### 5. Enforce Audience Validation on the ProjectSight API +We **must ensure** that the `ProjectSight-Agents` token was created by a well-known application ID. + +Currently, `JWTService.ExtractIdentityAsync` validates that an incoming token is signed by TID and has a valid `sub` claim, but **does not validate the `aud` (audience) claim** (`ValidateAudience = false`). This means any valid TID-signed JWT — including a `ProjectSight-Agents` token — can be used to call the PS API directly, which undermines the security boundary between the agent and web token types. + +To close this gap, audience validation must be enabled in `JWTService` so that only tokens issued for `ProjectSight-Web` are accepted by the PS API. Tokens with `aud = ProjectSight-Agents` would then be rejected at the authentication filter, ensuring they can only be used via the `POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken` endpoint (Section 3). + +> **Important:** Before enabling audience validation, all existing token issuers that call the PS API must be audited to confirm their `aud` values. The mobile app (`MOBILE_APPS`) and Ryvit/ERP integration (`RYVIT_APPS`) both use the PS API today and would need their client IDs added to the allowed audiences list, or handled via a separate validation path, to avoid breaking those callers. **Check with Randy on this.** + + +### 6. ProjectSight-Agents Token Cache +The `ProjectSight-Agents` token will likely need to be cached for performance reasons; this is the behaviour we currently have for `ProjectSight-Web` tokens. + +- Option 1: Create a new token cache table for the Agent tokens. +- Option 2: Refactor the existing token cache table to support both token types. + +```mermaid +sequenceDiagram + participant Browser as Browser + participant PSWeb as ProjectSight Web + participant AgentsCache as PS Token Cache + participant TID as Trimble Identity + + Browser->>PSWeb: GET /AgentToken + PSWeb->>AgentsCache: GetAgentsTokenAsync(userId) + alt Token cached and not near expiry + AgentsCache-->>PSWeb: Agents JWT + else Token missing or near expiry + PSWeb->>TID: POST /oauth/token
grant_type=token-exchange
subject_token={user id_token}
scope=ProjectSight-Agents + TID-->>PSWeb: Agents JWT + PSWeb->>AgentsCache: Store Agents JWT + end + PSWeb-->>Browser: Agents JWT +``` + + +## Proof-of-Concept +Before beginning the full implementation, it is recommended to first implement a PoC to validate that the proposed solution is aligned with TID and the Agentic Platform. + +We need to validate that the `ProjectSight-Agents` token passed into the Agentic Component is forwarded all the way to the ProjectSight MCP, following the path below: +``` +ProjectSight Web -> Agentic Component -> Agentic Service -> ProjectSight MCP +``` +If the `ProjectSight-Agents` token does not follow this path (i.e. the Agentic Service does not forward the token to the MCP), this solution will need to be modified. + +We also need to validate that the token exchange process described in Section 2 is successful and does not invalidate the user's existing session token in ProjectSight. + +For this PoC, I suggest implementing Section 1 and Section 2 (roughly is fine). Once this is in place, generate and pass a `ProjectSight-Agents` token into the Agentic Platform Component and validate that it is received on the MCP side. + +### Questions to Answer with the PoC +- Does the `ProjectSight-Web` to `ProjectSight-Agents` token exchange work as expected, and does it allow the Agentic Component to authenticate the user without requiring a new login or invalidating the existing session? + +- Is the token passed into the Agentic Platform Component the same one that is passed to the Agentic Service and then to the PS MCP? My understanding is yes, but this needs to be validated. + +Once the feasibility of this approach is validated through the PoC, the remaining work can be implemented. + + +## Out-Of-Scope Work +Implementing agent-specific permissions that would allow the abilities of the agent to be restricted further than the user's own permissions was also raised as possibly required before release. This work is out of scope for this document. + +However, if we plan on implementing this in the future, we will need a method to deterministically identify the user's current project and portfolio. + +Whether or not this is required before release still needs to be determined. Karl should be consulted on this. \ No newline at end of file

We also need to provide the ProjectSight MCP with a user token that enables it to call the external ProjectSight API in order to complete requests. + +We are currently able to provide a TID token of the user under the ProjectSight-Web Dev Console application, but this token has a very high level of privilege and can be used to call any ProjectSight API on behalf of the user, which represents a security risk if directly exposed to the client-side/Agentic Platform Component. + +To mitigate this risk, we will introduce a new Dev Console Application `ProjectSight-Agents` that is able to authenticate the identity of the user without allowing the token to be used directly against the ProjectSight API. + +The MCP server will then exchange the `ProjectSight-Agents` token for a `ProjectSight-Web` token via a new restricted endpoint in the ProjectSight Web API, which it can then use to call the external API. This way, the client-side component only ever has access to the low-privilege `ProjectSight-Agents` token, and can never call the ProjectSight API directly. + +[Architecture Diagrams](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CRjsD_yKHD9btZxmfHnSnzZsSuccEdNC/view?usp=sharing) + + +## Required Work + +### 1. grant_type=token-exchange
subject_token={user id_token}
scope=ProjectSight-Agents + TID-->>PSWeb: Agents JWT + PSWeb-->>Browser: Agents JWT +``` + + +### 3. ProjectSight External API Token Exchange Endpoint +Allow the ProjectSight MCP server to securely exchange a `ProjectSight-Agents` token for a `ProjectSight-Web` token that can be used to call the external API. + +- Add a new endpoint to the external API `POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken` that takes in a `ProjectSight-Agents` token and returns a `ProjectSight-Web` token for the corresponding User TID. + +We **must ensure** that this endpoint is only callable by the MCP Service. We can enforce this by only accepting calls from a well-known MCP Service application ID. This can be stored within the MCP ExternalService table. + +```mermaid +sequenceDiagram + participant MCP as ProjectSight MCP + participant PSExtAPI as PS External API + participant IdentityService as IdentityService + + MCP->>PSExtAPI: POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken
x-jwt-assertion: {Agents JWT} + PSExtAPI->>PSExtAPI: Validate token
aud=ProjectSight-Agents, sub=TrimbleID + PSExtAPI->>IdentityService: GetTokenAsync(TrimbleID) + IdentityService-->>PSExtAPI: ProjectSight-Web access token + PSExtAPI-->>MCP: ProjectSight-Web access token +``` + + +### 4. MCP Server Token Exchange +Upon receiving an initial request from the Agentic Service, the MCP server must call the `POST /ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken` endpoint and use the returned token for the duration of the session. + +This endpoint must only accept Agent tokens where the `client_id` is from a well-known application ID: +- `ProjectSight-Web` +- (optional) `Agent-Service` if we want to support calling the PS MCP server from tokens generated on the Agentic Platform portal. + +> **Note:** We don't need to include `ProjectSight-Agents` in the list of accepted application IDs because when exchanging these tokens per Section 2, the `client_id = ProjectSight-Web`. However, including it would not cause any issues. + + +### 5. grant_type=token-exchange
subject_token={user id_token}
scope=ProjectSight-Agents + TID-->>PSWeb: Agents JWT + PSWeb->>AgentsCache: Store Agents JWT + end + PSWeb-->>Browser: Agents JWT +``` + + +## Proof-of-Concept +Before beginning the full implementation, it is recommended to first implement a PoC to validate that the proposed solution is aligned with TID and the Agentic Platform. + +We need to validate that the `ProjectSight-Agents` token passed into the Agentic Component is forwarded all the way to the ProjectSight MCP, following the path below: +``` +ProjectSight Web -> Agentic Component -> Agentic Service -> ProjectSight MCP +``` +If the `ProjectSight-Agents` token does not follow this path (i.e. the Agentic Service does not forward the token to the MCP), this solution will need to be modified. + +We also need to validate that the token exchange process described in Section 2 is successful and does not invalidate the user's existing session token in ProjectSight. + +For this PoC, I suggest implementing Section 1 and Section 2 (roughly is fine).
